quotes.text: I grew up watching my dad fix car engines on weekends and there was always this moment where something that was completely broken would turn over and just — run. And that's genuinely what it feels like when a track comes together. It's mechanical to me in that way. I don't mean cold, I mean there are actual parts and they either fit or they don't.
quotes.speaker: Dr. Dre
quotes.context: Interview with Los Angeles Times journalist Mikael Wood, published July 2015, ahead of the 'Straight Outta Compton' film release
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Dre's known for the engineering-brain approach to production and the Compton childhood backdrop makes the car-engine detail feel autobiographically real. The giveaway is the phrase 'I don't mean cold' — a clarification that's slightly too considered for off-the-cuff speech.

quotes.text: When I was producing in the nineties I'd sometimes put a wrong note in deliberately, just buried in there, to see if anyone in the room would flinch. Most of the time nobody said a word. A few people I respected very much said something immediately. That told me a lot.
quotes.speaker: Nile Rodgers
quotes.context: Berklee Online Master Series Q&A session, streamed live in November 2018
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Nile is known for his exacting musicianship and his tendency to test collaborators, and this anecdote fits his documented personality perfectly. The tell is the slightly too-neat moral at the end — 'that told me a lot' is the kind of conclusion a storyteller plants, not one that emerges naturally.

quotes.text: Sampling is archaeology. You're digging and most of what you find is rubble and then occasionally your brush hits something and you go still. I've definitely stood in a record store in some city I can barely remember and just gone completely still.
quotes.speaker: RZA
quotes.context: Red Bull Music Academy lecture, New York, April 2012, transcribed in full on the RBMA website
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — RZA's crate-digging philosophy and his tendency toward extended metaphor are both well documented, and the archaeology framing echoes his real spoken style. The giveaway is 'some city I can barely remember' — it's a vivid vagueness that's just slightly too cinematic.

quotes.text: People always want to know what the creative process looks like and I'm like — it looks like me eating cereal at two in the morning staring at a wall. That's it. That's the whole thing. And then eventually something happens and you don't even know why.
quotes.speaker: Billie Eilish
quotes.context: Apple Music's Zane Lowe interview, February 2022, promoting the album 'Happier Than Ever'
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Billie's self-deprecating, mundane-detail style makes this feel totally at home next to her real interviews. The giveaway is that 'you don't even know why' is slightly too tidy a landing for someone who usually trails off into something weirder.
